text: Garment. Hebrew, "cloak," (Sanctius) such as were worn by people under no disgrace, Baruch 4:20. Thamar had one very costly, 2 Kings 13:18. (Calmet)<hr>Justice, or mercy. God is bound to fulfill his gracious promises. (Worthington)
His. Greek, "thy." Christ, the sun of justice, would effect this, Isaias 60:1.
Piety. Thus shall Jerusalem be distinguished after the captivity; but still more so when Christ shall appear, and give peace to his Church, after the ages of persecution.
Arise. Baruch 4:36., and Isaias 52:2.<hr>God. They remember his law with pleasure.

Children. Greek, "the throne." The return of the captives was a triumph, Isaias 46:20., and 49:22. They had abundance of horses, etc., 1 Esdras 2:66. Darius gave them a convoy of 1000 horsemen. (3 Esdras 5:2.) (Josephus, Antiquities 11:4.)
Diligently, without stumbling under the pillar of God's protection, Isaias 40:3. (Calmet) Ad vos qua veniet, tumidi subsidite montes Et faciles curvis vallibus este viae. (Ovid, Am. 2:16.)
Woods. This is a poetical description. They shall pass through the sands of Arabia, as if they were under a delightful shade.
